title: Ensure that the --basic-auth-file argument is not set

input_row.title: Ensure that the --basic-auth-file argument is not set
input_row.rationale: Basic authentication uses plaintext credentials for authentication. Currently, the basic authentication credentials last indefinitely, and the password cannot be changed without restarting the federation API server. The basic authentication is currently supported for convenience. Hence, basic authentication should not be used.
input_row.audit: Run the following command: ps -ef | grep federation-apiserver Verify that the --basic-auth-file argument does not exist.
input_row.remediation: Follow the documentation and configure alternate mechanisms for authentication. Then, edit the deployment specs and remove "--basic-auth-file=<filename>". kubectl edit deployments federation-apiserver-deployment -- namespace=federation-system Impact: You will have to configure and use alternate authentication mechanisms such as tokens and certificates. Username and password for basic authentication could no more be used. Default Value: By default, basic authentication is not set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/federation-apiserver/ 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/master/federation/manifests/fe deration-apiserver-deployment.yaml  187 | P a g e 3. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/deployment/
input_row.impact: You will have to configure and use alternate authentication mechanisms such as tokens and certificates. Username and password for basic authentication could no more be used. Default Value: By default, basic authentication is not set.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_federation_api_server_basic_auth_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the Kubernetes API for the Deployment (or Pod, if not managed by a Deployment) running the federation-apiserver in the federation-system namespace.
- Use kubectl or the Kubernetes API to retrieve the PodSpec for federation-apiserver containers.
- Inspect the container command and args fields for the presence of the --basic-auth-file argument.
- Pass Criteria: The --basic-auth-file argument is NOT present in any container spec for the federation-apiserver.
- Fail Criteria: The --basic-auth-file argument is present in any container spec for the federation-apiserver.
- Example kubectl command:
  ```
  kubectl -n federation-system get deployments -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.metadata.name | test("federation-apiserver")) | .spec.template.spec.containers[].args'
  ```
  Or, for direct inspection:
  ```
  kubectl -n federation-system get deployment federation-apiserver-deployment -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[*].args}'
  ```
- The check should ensure that none of the args or command fields include --basic-auth-file. If found, flag as non-compliant. If absent, mark as compliant.
